mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rate increases the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is monotonic in the level of the rate.
evidence_refs: fact:Standard discount rate model of equity pricing
failure_condition: Spearman rank correlation between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward SPX returns is non-negative over any 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.03 over full sample.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 6%, the Federal Reserve is forced to maintain an aggressively tight policy stance, eroding real corporate earnings and consumer purchasing power, which depresses equity prices over the following week.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical negative equity returns during high inflation regimes (1970s, 2022)
failure_condition: When triggered, average 5-day forward SPX return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous decline in retail sales (RSAFS) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a broad weakening of the domestic economy, reducing corporate revenue and employment, which forecasts negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Comovement of consumer demand and labor market during recessions
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint signal (e.g., PCA first component or equally-weighted composite) with 60-day forward returns is below 0.04 or has same sign of direction less than 60% of months.

mechanism_narrative: Falling housing starts during a monetary tightening cycle amplify the negative impact of rising rates, as the housing sector is the most interest-rate-sensitive component of demand, leading to spillovers into financials and consumer sentiment, which drag equity returns lower over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are a leading indicator of economic downturns
failure_condition: In periods when FEDFUNDS > 2.5%, the correlation between HOUST changes and 20-day forward SPX returns is not significantly negative, or Rank IC < 0.03 by rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output, higher capacity utilization, and stronger corporate earnings, which supports a positive outlook for the S&P 500 index over a monthly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a coincident indicator of GDP growth
failure_condition: Spearman rank correlation between INDPRO monthly change and 20-day forward SPX returns is non-positive over any 3-year window, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate surpasses 5%, the labor market is substantially weakened, signaling a transition into recession or early slowdown, which historically depresses corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:~5% unemployment has been a threshold for recessionary signals (Sahm rule)
failure_condition: When triggered, average 20-day forward SPX return is not significantly negative (one-sided p > 0.10) or the fraction of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of persistently high CPI inflation, an elevated federal funds rate, and rising unemployment creates a stagflationary environment where traditional monetary tools lose effectiveness, deeply compressing equity risk appetite and leading to pronounced negative returns over a monthly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Stagflation episodes (1974, 1980, 2022) correlate with negative equity returns
failure_condition: The joint z-score composite of the three proxies shows rank IC < 0.05 for 20-day forward returns, or the composite direction is positive in more than 45% of months triggered.

mechanism_narrative: Above-trend GDP growth (real GDP growth exceeding 3% annualized) in a late-cycle expansion signals overheating and a rising probability of a correction, as capacity constraints tighten and the Federal Reserve eventually reacts, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Late-cycle overheating often precedes bear markets (2000, 2007)
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion (real GDP above potential and low unemployment)
failure_condition: When real GDP growth is > 3%, the average 60-day forward SPX return is not negative (t-test p < 0.10) or the negative hit rate is below 60%.
